selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a clear understanding of the sources of fake content and how they operate. This includes recognizing that fake content can come from both external actors and internal actors, such as bots and hackers, as well as individuals who intentionally or unintentionally spread misinformation. Once you have identified the source of the fake content, you can take steps to mitigate its impact by using fact-checking tools and sources, as well as by reporting any fake content to the platform or service where it was posted. Additionally, it is important to be mindful of your own behavior on social media and to avoid spreading misinformation yourself, as this can contribute to a culture of distrust and confusion. By taking these steps, you can help to create a more informed and responsible social media environment.","reasoning_summary":"• Identify sources of fake content • Use fact-checking tools and sources • Report fake content to platform or service • Be mindful of own behavior on social media • Create a more informed and responsible social media environment ","tone":"educated , responsible , trustworthy","citations":["<source name or org>"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a clear understanding of the sources of fake content and how they operate. This includes recognizing that fake content can come from both external actors and internal actors, such as bots and hackers, as well as individuals who intentionally or unintentionally spread misinformation. Once you have identified the source of the fake content, you can take steps to mitigate its impact by using fact-checking tools and sources, as well as by reporting any fake content to the platform or service where it was posted. Additionally, it is important to be mindful of your own behavior on social media and to avoid spreading misinformation yourself, as this can contribute to a culture of distrust and confusion. By taking these steps, you can help to create a more informed and responsible social media environment.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: • Identify sources of fake content • Use fact-checking tools and sources • Report fake content to platform or service • Be mindful of own behavior on social media • Create a more informed and responsible social media environment
